topStories.title: US deploys 5,000 additional Marines and Sailors to Middle East amid intensifying Iran conflict: Report
topStories.description: The deployment, requested by CENTCOM, aims to provide rapid crisis response, protect embassies, evacuate civilians, and secure key entry points amid the ongoing conflict.

topStories.title: US Deploys B-2 Bombers For Operation Against Iran
topStories.description: The United States Central Command (CENTCOM) on Friday said B-2 stealth bombers have taken off to carry out a mission as part of Operation Epic Fury, aimed at "eliminating threats" posed by the Iranian...

topStories.title: US offers $10 million reward for information on Mojtaba Khamenei, key Iranian leaders
topStories.description: The announcement targets key Iranian figures, including Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali Larijani, Maj. Gen. Yahya Rahim Safavi, and others amid heightened Middle East tensions following US and Israeli strikes.

topStories.title: US Sending 2,500 Marines To Middle East As Iran War Intensifies
topStories.description: About 2,500 US Marines are being deployed to the Middle East as American and Israeli strikes keep pounding Iran and the Islamic Republic keeps attacking Persian Gulf shipping and energy...

topStories.title: Opposition leaders press government, Lok Sabha Speaker to revoke the suspension of 8 MPs
topStories.description: The eight MPs, including seven from the Congress, have been barred from attending the remainder of the Budget Session

topStories.title: ‘You don’t need to worry about it’: Pete Hegseth on Iran's Hormuz shipping threat
topStories.description: The Strait of Hormuz has come under global focus as tensions rise following joint strikes by United States and Israel on Iran. Commercial ships in the strait and the Persian Gulf have recently faced missile and drone attack risks linked to Iran.

topStories.title: ‘Improper purpose’: US federal judge quashes subpoenas against Jerome Powell, says 'govt tried to pressure on rates'
topStories.description: The Judge wrote that evidence suggests that the government served the subpoenas ‘on the Board to pressure its Chair into voting for lower interest rates or resigning’

topStories.title: Cuba confirms talks with Trump officials; President Miguel Diaz-Canel seeks solutions amid severe energy crisis
topStories.description: Oil shipments from Venezuela to Cuba were halted after the United States attacked Venezuela and arrested President Nicolás Maduro, deepening Cuba’s energy crisis.

topStories.title: Menstrual leave law not in women’s best interest: SC
topStories.description: The Supreme Court declined to mandate menstrual leave, citing potential job market issues, and urged the government to consider a uniform policy.

dailyNews.title: Trump says US raid 'obliterated' military targets on Iran's 'crown jewel' Kharg Island
dailyNews.description: Trump says US raid obliterated military targets on Irans crown jewel Kharg Island

dailyNews.title: US judge nixes two subpoenas against Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell
dailyNews.description: Judge James Boasberg ruled US prosecutors under Trump produced 'essentially zero evidence' to justify the subpoenas.

dailyNews.title: Qatar’s interior minister says security situation ‘stable’ amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad says Qatar will 'not hesitate' to ensure its stability as US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

dailyNews.title: Bolivian authorities capture drug kingpin Sebastian Marset in police raid
dailyNews.description: Marset was transferred into the custody of the United States, which has pushed for more aggressive antidrug tactics.

dailyNews.title: US Offers $10 Million Reward For Information On Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali Larijani, Top Irania
dailyNews.description: Reward announced hours after these leaders attended al-Quds demonstrations in Iran

dailyNews.title: Rs 92,47,48,000: The Bounty US Has Set For Information On Mojtaba Khamenei
dailyNews.description: The United States has offered a reward of up to $10 million or Rs 92,47,48,000 at current exchange rates for information on Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, and several other senior...

dailyNews.title: Brazil pulls visa of Trump adviser who asked to visit Bolsonaro in prison
dailyNews.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva says Darren Beattie was 'prohibited from visiting' Bolsonaro in prison.

dailyNews.title: What rats do’: US says Iranian leaders hiding as leaders appear in Tehran
dailyNews.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Iran’s leadership was “desperate and hiding,” after US-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: Haryana Rajya Sabha polls: Congress MLAs reach Himachal Pradesh to prevent cross-voting
dailyNews.description: After CLP meet, about 30 MLAs leave for Shimla; party insiders say the move is meant to keep the flock together ahead of three-cornered election; Congress holds 37 seats and needs 31 votes to send its candidate

dailyNews.title: Brazil’s jailed ex-President Jair Bolsonaro hospitalised with bronchopneumonia; condition serious
dailyNews.description: The 70-year-old was taken to the hospital early Friday (March 13) after experiencing high fever, chills and a drop in oxygen saturation, doctors said.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine, EU allies slam US decision to roll back Russia oil sanctions
dailyNews.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says move 'does not help peace' as Europe reels from soaring energy prices linked to Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine finds new role as protector of US, Gulf allies amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: Having abandoned Ukraine, the US has sought Kyiv's support in defence from Iranian drones.

dailyNews.title: Sheriff in Nancy Guthrie case believes they know kidnapper's motive
dailyNews.description: The 84-year-old mother of US TV presenter Savannah Guthrie was kidnapped 41 days ago.

dailyNews.title: Ukraine and allies fear easing Russian sanctions will prolong war
dailyNews.description: Ukraine's president says the US decision "certainly does not help achieve peace".

topStories.title: Trump welcomes PRCA, WPRA champions at White House in return of rodeo tradition
topStories.description: Rodeo buckle winners visited the White House for the first time in more than four decades when President Donald Trump hosted PRCA and WPRA world champions.

topStories.title: White House erupts over CNN report claiming Trump team underestimated Iran response on Hormuz
topStories.description: White House press secretary denies CNN report claiming Trump team unprepared for potential Iranian closure of Strait of Hormuz, calling it "fake news."

topStories.title: Trump Kennedy Center's new leader revealed as Ric Grenell exits top role
topStories.description: Ric Grenell is stepping down as the Trump Kennedy Center president as the venue shifts focus to major renovations, with facilities VP Matt Floca taking over leadership.

topStories.title: Cuban president admits talks with Trump admin as fuel blockade chokes domestic energy supply and economy
topStories.description: Cuba confirmed talks with the Trump administration amid economic crisis following Venezuela oil cutoff as President Miguel Díaz-Canel cites aim to resolve differences.

topStories.title: Trump says US ‘obliterated’ targets in strike on key Iranian oil hub
topStories.description: Trump said the U.S. bombed Iran’s Kharg Island, a key Iranian oil export hub, and warned against interference in the Strait of Hormuz.

topStories.title: Boxer Jake Paul hints at future run for office in interview with Trump
topStories.description: Trump predicts Jake Paul will run for political office 'in the not-too-distant future' and offers complete endorsement during Kentucky campaign rally.

dailyNews.description: Trump predicts Jake Paul will run for political office 'in the not-too-distant future' and offers complete endorsement during Kentucky campaign rally.

dailyNews.title: Judge Quashes Justice Dept.’s Subpoenas of Fed, Crippling Its Pursuit of Trump’s Rivals
dailyNews.description: Judge James E. Boasberg derided the U.S. attorney’s office in Washington for pursuing a case against Jerome H. Powell that appeared to be motivated by President Trump’s desire for vengeance.

dailyNews.title: Judge Temporarily Blocks Trump Demand for Student Race Data
dailyNews.description: A group of Democratic attorneys general had sued to overturn the Trump administration’s new policy that demanded the past seven years of student application data.

dailyNews.title: More Marines and Warships Being Sent to Middle East, U.S. Officials Say
dailyNews.description: Iran’s response to days of aerial bombardment and long-range artillery strikes has proved more resilient than Trump administration officials anticipated.

dailyNews.title: Trump Softens Call for Protesters to Take Over Iran
dailyNews.description: President Trump said protesters risk getting shot “right through the head,” a change in tone from his earlier comments that Iranians must seize the chance to take over their government.

dailyNews.title: His Harvard Lab Was Thriving. Then Came the Cuts.
dailyNews.description: Will Mair, who studies aging, lost almost all his research funds when the White House cracked down on Harvard. He was wholly unprepared for the upheaval that followed.

dailyNews.title: When a President Gets Addicted to Regime Change
dailyNews.description: Venezuela gave Trump a taste of success. This isn’t the first time an American president has gotten hooked on overthrowing foreign governments.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Dr. Oz pledges to tackle hospice fraud: "Do not steal from the American people"
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration's Medicare boss reacts to CBS News investigation into California's hospice fraud problems.

dailyNews.title: Jim Clyburn seeking 18th term in Congress at 85 years old
dailyNews.description: Democratic Rep. Jim Clyburn of South Carolina announced this week that he will seek an 18th term in Congress, despite a years-long push for generational change within the Democratic Party. The 85-year-old says he wants to do what's best for his state and the country. Fin Gómez, CBS News exclusive director of White House and politics, has more.
